codeReview.summary: Implementation is correct. Promotion thresholds for patterns (≥5 confirmations with confidence >0.6) and conventions (≥10 confirmations with confidence >0.8) are properly implemented with 1.5x retrieval rank boost for promoted items. All 15 promotion tests pass. Boundary conditions (exactly 0.6 and exactly 0.8) are correctly handled with > comparison.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The get_retrieval_rank_boost() method is implemented and tested, but the recall module (recall.rs) does not use AntiPattern or Convention for retrieval - it uses conversation logs. The 'higher retrieval rank' behavior is not actually integrated into the retrieval flow yet.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: grep 'AntiPattern|Convention' recall.rs returns no matches. get_retrieval_rank_boost only appears in pattern_learning.rs tests and implementation.

summary: Implementation passes review. Promotion thresholds correctly implemented with proper boundary conditions (≥5 conf/>0.6 for patterns, ≥10 conf/>0.8 for rules). All tests pass. Non-blocking observation: retrieval integration not yet implemented - boost method exists but recall.rs doesn't use AntiPattern/Convention.
